response: {"name":"eCLIP-seq data processing","description":"End-to-end computational pipeline for processing eCLIP-seq libraries, including demultiplexing, adapter trimming, genome alignment, PCR duplicate removal, peak calling, and downstream analyses such as motif finding, downsampling, and SMInput normalization.","data_type":"sequencing","raw_data_source":null,"steps":[{"step_number":1,"description":"Standard HiSeq demultiplexing followed by inline barcode demultiplexing of eCLIP libraries using custom scripts; the random-mer (UMI) is appended to the read name for later use.","input_data":"Raw FASTQ reads from HiSeq sequencing","output_data":"Demultiplexed FASTQ reads with random-mer appended to read names","software":"Custom scripts (Illumina HiSeq demultiplexing + in-house inline barcode demultiplexing)","software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":2,"description":"Adapter trimming of demultiplexed reads; reads shorter than 18 bp after trimming are discarded.","input_data":"Demultiplexed FASTQ reads","output_data":"Adapter-trimmed FASTQ reads (≥18 bp)","software":"cutadapt","software_version":"1.9.dev1","parameters":{"min_length":"18"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":3,"description":"Reads are first mapped against human repetitive elements in RepBase to segregate repeat-mapping reads for separate analysis.","input_data":"Adapter-trimmed FASTQ reads","output_data":"Repeat-mapped reads (segregated) and unmapped reads passed to genomic alignment","software":"STAR","software_version":"2.4.0i","parameters":{"reference":"RepBase v18.05 (human elements)"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":4,"description":"Non-repeat reads are mapped against the full human genome (hg19) including a splice junction database.","input_data":"Non-repeat FASTQ reads","output_data":"Aligned BAM files (uniquely mapping reads against hg19)","software":"STAR","software_version":"2.4.0i","parameters":{"genome":"hg19","annotation":"splice junction database"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":5,"description":"PCR duplicate removal using a custom script that removes duplicates sharing identical Read1 start position, Read2 start position, and random-mer sequence, yielding 'Usable' reads.","input_data":"Uniquely mapping BAM files","output_data":"PCR-deduplicated 'Usable' reads BAM files","software":"Custom PCR duplicate removal script","software_version":null,"parameters":{"deduplication_criteria":"identical Read1 start position, Read2 start position, and random-mer sequence"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":6,"description":"For eCLIP datasets with multiple inline barcodes, usable reads from each barcode are merged into a single dataset prior to peak calling.","input_data":"Per-barcode deduplicated 'Usable' reads BAM files","output_data":"Merged 'Usable' reads BAM file","software":"Custom scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":7,"description":"Cluster/peak identification is performed on usable reads using CLIPper, considering Read 2 only (enriched for termination at the crosslink site).","input_data":"Merged 'Usable' reads BAM file","output_data":"CLIPper peak/cluster calls","software":"CLIPper","software_version":"1.0","parameters":{"-s":"hg19","-o":"true","--bonferroni":"true","--superlocal":"true","--threshold-method":"binomial","--save-pickle":"true","read_considered":"Read 2 only"},"code_reference":"https://github.com/YeoLab/clipper/releases/tag/1.0"},{"step_number":8,"description":"All genome browser tracks are RPM-normalized against the total number of usable reads in each dataset for visualization on the UCSC Genome Browser.","input_data":"Usable reads BAM files","output_data":"RPM-normalized genome browser tracks","software":"Custom normalization scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{"normalization":"RPM (reads per million usable reads)"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":9,"description":"Downsampling analysis performed by 100 iterations of randomly permuting uniquely mapped reads, selecting the top N reads, and applying PCR duplicate removal to identify usable reads at each depth.","input_data":"Uniquely mapped BAM files","output_data":"Usable read counts at various downsampled depths (100 iterations each)","software":"Custom downsampling scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{"iterations":"100","selection":"top N reads per iteration","note":"For iCLIP with multiple cDNA-size libraries, only library with highest percent usable was used"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":10,"description":"De novo motif finding for RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP clusters using HOMER's findMotifs program, comparing cluster sequences against background regions matched by genic region type.","input_data":"CLIPper cluster sequences (eCLIP and iCLIP for RBFOX2)","output_data":"De novo RNA motifs","software":"HOMER findMotifs","software_version":null,"parameters":{"-p":"4","-rna":"true","-S":"10","-len":"5,6,7,8,9","background":"3 random same-sized regions per cluster matched by genic region type (introns, 3′UTRs, etc.)"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":11,"description":"Cluster location pie charts are generated by counting the total number of bases covered by peaks for each genic region type.","input_data":"CLIPper peak/cluster calls with genic region annotations","output_data":"Cluster location pie charts","software":"Custom annotation scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":12,"description":"For RBFOX2 RNase I shearing analysis, each cluster is annotated by overlapping genic region type and peaks per region are counted.","input_data":"CLIPper clusters from RBFOX2 RNase I shearing eCLIP","output_data":"Peak counts per genic region type","software":"Custom annotation scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{"annotation_priority":"same region priority order as region-level analysis"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":13,"description":"For motif enrichment analysis before and after SMInput normalization (RBFOX2, PUM2, TARDBP, TRA2A), CLIPper-identified clusters are extended to a minimum of 100 nt centered around the cluster midpoint.","input_data":"CLIPper cluster coordinates for RBFOX2, PUM2, TARDBP, and TRA2A","output_data":"Extended cluster regions (≥100 nt) for motif enrichment analysis","software":"Custom scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{"extension":"minimum 100 nt centered on CLIPper cluster midpoint"},"code_reference":null}],"figures_produced":["Figure 1","Figure 1a","Figure 1b","Figure
